content.full_text:
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-8873

DISTRICT OFFICE
1145 TAMPA AVENUE
SUITE 215
NORTHRIDGE, CA 91326 AUG 9 '90
TELEPHONE: (818) 368-8717

COMMITTEES:
JUDICIARY
VICE CHAIRMAN
BANKING AND COMMERCE
BUDGET & FISCAL REVIEW
EDUCATION
INSURANCE: CLAIMS AND
CORPORATIONS
SELECT COMMITTEES:
BORDER ISSUES GROUP
CALIFORNIA COMPETITIVENESS AND
GOVERNMENTAL EFFICIENCY
MOTION PICTURE, TELEVISION,
RECORDING & RECORDING
INDUSTRIES
SUBSTANCE
JOINT COMMITTEES:
FIRE POLICE EMERGENCY AND
DISASTER SERVICES
ORGANIZED CRIME
RULES COMMITTEE
PRISON CONSTRUCTION AND
OPERATIONS

ED DAVIS
NINETEENTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT
REPRESENTING PORTIONS OF LOS ANGELES,
SANTA BARBARA, AND VENTURA COUNTIES

California State Senate
September 14, 1990

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

At the suggestion of one of your attorneys, Deanne Stone, I am
writing this letter to request the FPPC's advice regarding the
use of Senator Davis' campaign funds for a conference he is
planning this fall in his district.

Senator Davis has frequently carried bills for local public
agencies and believes that these individuals will greatly
benefit from a conference designed to educate and inform them
on how to lobby successfully in Sacramento.

The Program will begin with a continental breakfast in the
morning, and then there will be two panel discussions. The
first panel will be comprised of lobbyists from Sacramento and
the second panel will consist of local government lobbyists.
These participants will discuss their experiences in
Sacramento. These panel discussions will be followed by a
reception, then a lunch where Senator Davis will be the
speaker. The program will run from 8:30 a.m. to 1.30 p.m.

Among those invited to attend will be all cities in the
district, the Ventura and Santa Barbara County Board of
Supervisors, Special Districts (including
Community College Districts), and if space allows local citizen
activists. The cost for the invitations, conference room
rental, and meals should be no greater than $35 per person.

September 14, 1990
Scott Hallabrin-FPPC
Page Two

It would be appreciated if the FPPC would inform us if Senator
Davis is allowed to use his campaign funds for the following
purposes:
    1.  The hotel and meals for all of the
        participants.
    2.  The meals of each participant on
        the panels, along with transportation and
        room accommodations if required.
    3.  The overhead cost, such as the invitations
        and postage.

We have reserved October 23, November 2 or November 9 at the
Woodland Hills Marriott for the conference. We will need to
make final arrangements by October 1st. Although I recognize
that you have until October 15 to furnish your opinion, it
would be greatly appreciated if you could expedite this matter.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

HUNT BRALY
Administrative Assistant
